lines.text: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is eating breakfast as Chandler comes out of his bedroom, ripping his coat in the process.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! That ripped! That ripped real nice!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How many times do I have to tell you! Ya, turn and sliiiide! Y'know, turn and slide.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't turn and slide, you throw it out! I'm tired of having to get a tetanus shot every time I get dressed!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, we're not throwing it out! I built this thing with my own hands!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, how about we, how 'bout we sell it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. But, you're gonna have to tell them. (He opens the cabinet to reveal the chick and the duck living inside with Christmas lights and a disco ball as decorations.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (nods his head) Do you mind if we stick you in another cabinet? (to Joey) They seem all right with it!
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Ross are there. Monica is checking the messages.

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: (on machine) Hey Monica, it's Chip.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yesss!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who's Chip?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Shhh!

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: (on machine) Good runnin' into you at the bank today, so ah, here's my number, 555-9323. Give me a call. Later.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chip, is Chip Matthews.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The guy who took Rachel to the prom? Why is he calling you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 'Cause I ran into him at the bank, he is still soo cute.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica, you're so lucky! He's like the most popular guy in school!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know!! (calls him) (on phone) Chip? Hi! It's Monica. (listens) 'Kay. (listens) 'Kay. (listens) Okay. (listens) Okay, good-bye. (hangs up) Oh my God, we just had the best conversation!! (goes into her room as Rachel enters)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) I was just leaving.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good! 'Cause I've got a product report to read, it's like eight pages, I hope I don't fall asleep.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why? Did you write it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sees Chip's phone number) Wow! Look at that, Chip Matthews called. I wonder what he wants?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well ah, actually...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (interrupting) I bet he sensed that I was ready to have sex with another guy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, umm, why don't you give him a call?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (picks up phone) Are you sure you wanna hear this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on phone) Chip! Hi, it's Rachel. (listens) Rachel Green. Yeah, umm, you left me a message. (listens) Yes you did, my roommate wrote it down. (listens) Monica Geller. (listens) Ohh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (whispering in her ear) Oh, that's right! He called to ask out Monica! That-that's gotta be embarrassing!
Central Perk, Phoebe is finishing up a song.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (screaming, angrily) ...DUMB, DRUNKEN, BITCH!!! (applause) (happily) Thank you, thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (reading the newspaper) Hey, here's a question; where did you guys get the finest oak East of the Mississippi?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-huh, first you tell us where you got the prettiest lace in all the land.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm reading your ad.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Looks good, uh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (reading the ad) Stunning entertainment center. Fine, (pause) fine Italian craftsmanship. (Joey is very proud of himself)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, you guys are selling the entertainment center?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why? I love that thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You want it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh, Gepeto, $5,000 dollars? Are you insane?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, the ad alone cost 300 bucks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right look, I'm changing it to 50 bucks, or your best offer.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What kind of profit is that?! And you call yourself an accountant.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looks at him) Nooo.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. What do you do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe you don't know what I do for a living!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I actually don't know...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good, so do I

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Something to do with numbers?
[Cut to someone entering Central Perk which lets a cat in. The cat then runs over to Phoebe's guitar case and starts sniffing around.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! No! Shoo! Kitty! No! No-no-no! Shoo! Come on, you! (goes over and picks up the cat) Come on. Crazy. (looks into the cat's eyes) Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nothing. Nothing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just, I just have this really strong feeling that this cat is my Mother.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You mean the mom you met in Montauk. She was a cat?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no-no, she was a human lady. This is the spirit of my Mom Lily, the one who killed herself.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you sure she's in the cat, or have you been taking your grandma's glycoma medicine again?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No Dr. Skeptismo! I'm sure. First of all, okay, there's the feeling. (Chandler shrugs) Okay, and for another, how about the fact that she went into my guitar case which is lined with orange felt. (Rachel nods her head in unsure agreement) My Mother's favourite fish is Orange Roughy... (Joey thinks he understands, but then nods that he has no clue.) Cats....like....fish! (Ross and Rachel are totally lost) (to the cat) Hi, Mommy. (Rachel covers her mouth, in an "Oh my God." gesture) Oh, I haven't seen this smile in 17 years!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Chandler) Dude, Phoebe's mom has got a huge peni...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting) Let it go!!
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is working as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, when were you gonna tell me that you're going out with Chip Matthews?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now? Is it okay if I go out with Chip Matthews?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nooo! It's not okay! I can't believe you would want to after what he did to me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What, that little thing at the prom?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica! I couldn't find him for two hours! He was having sex with Amy Welch!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on, that was back in high school! How could that still bother you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean why, of all people would you want to go out with Chip?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, you and I went to different high schools...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, that doesn't help me, because we went to the same high school.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You went to one where you were popular, and you got to ride off Chip's motorcycle, and wear his letterman jacket. I went to one where I wore a band uniform they had to have specially made.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (shocked) They had to have that specially made?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It was a project for one of the Home Ec classes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (stunned) Oh my God, they told us that was for the mascot!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Back then, I thought that I would never, ever get the chance to go out with a Chip Matthews, and now he's-he's called me up and asked me out. And the fat girl inside of me really wants to go. I-I owe her this. I never let her eat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you go out with him. (goes over and hugs her)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, really?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Just, if it's possible, could you leave him somewhere and go have sex with another guy?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll try.
Monica and Rachel's, the next morning, they are all there eating breakfast.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So you guys having any luck getting rid of the entertainment center?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, there were a couple of calls last night, but ah, I don't think any of them are gonna work out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, Joey has a very careful screening process. Apparently, not everyone is qualified to own wood and nails.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (is struggling with the cat) Stop it! Stop it! She keeps squirming, trying to get away! Just like when she was alive.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So Pheebs, how long is your mom gonna be with us?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'm not sure. I mean, I guess until she y'know, gets used to the fact that there's y'know, a new mom. Y'know, I think she's worried that y'know, she's gonna, she's gonna be replaced. (to the cat, in a funny voice) Well, that's not gonna happen is it? Noo. (gets up) Okay, I have to return a call in the other room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why can't you use the phone in here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'm returning a call from a certain mom at the B-E-A-C-H. I just spelled the wrong word. (goes into Monica's room)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, guys, am I crazy, or does Phoebe's mom remind anyone of a cat?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, don't start.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, you-you can't tell me you actually believe that-that there's a woman inside that cat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I believe it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No you don't.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, I do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No you do-y'know what, you're not gonna suck me into this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh sure I am, because you always have to be right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I do not always have to be-okay, okay. (starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Jurassic Park could happen.
Ross wants to say something, but just smiles and leaves.
Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Chandler are showing a couple of guys (Tony and Peter) the entertainment center.

lines.speaker: Tony
lines.text: Wow! That's ah, that's pretty nice!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pretty nice?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You'll have to pardon my roommate, he wanted to marry this.

lines.speaker: Tony
lines.text: We don't have 50 bucks, but would you be willing to trade for it? We've got a canoe.
Joey jumps up in excitement and without turning around Chandler holds out his hand stopping him, and ushering him back into his seat. Joey sits down, dejected.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I, I really don't think we need a canoe.

lines.speaker: Tony
lines.text: You gotta take the canoe!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, just, just take the entertainment center, and then when you get home, throw the canoe away!

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: We're not throwing it away! I built that canoe! (starts to leave as Tony chases after him)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Peter) Good for you!!
Central Perk, Chandler, Joey, Monica, and Ross are there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) You guys, you're never gonna believe what I just found tacked up on a telephone pole! (She's holding a flyer.) Look kinda familiar?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (taking the flyer) Apparently Phoebe's mother also goes by the name Julio.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You guys, there's a little girl in Soho looking for this cat. I mean, you know what that means?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking at the flyer) Yeah-eah! 200 dollar reward, split five ways!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do we have to tell her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, we have to tell her!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, but it's made her so happy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (holds up a hand) Little girl misses her cat. (hold up the other hand) Crazy lady thinks her mother is in a cat. (gets up) Okay, y'know what, I have to go have dinner with my son, can I trust that when you see Phoebe, you will tell her.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: (disappointed) Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I hate when Ross is right!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He is right, isn't he?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what, I think this might be one of the times he's wrong.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: You think?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-no, he's right.
Commercial Break
Central Perk, the same scene is continued from before the break. With Joey, Monica, Rachel, and Chandler sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering, with Julio) Hi. (sits down in the chair)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, Pheebs, about your mom...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pause) How's that going?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So great. Oh, we took a nap today and my Mom fell asleep on my tummy and purred.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's so sweet. (pause) I'm gonna get some coffee. (gets up and leaves)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sliding into Joey's place on the couch to try and talk to Phoebe) Huh? What'd ya say Joe? I'll be right there. (gets up and joins Joey)
Rachel and Chandler slide into position.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just feel so, uhh.....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm coming already!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Jeez!
They both get up and leave Phoebe alone.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is reading at the kitchen table as there is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (running from the bathroom to her room, wearing only a towel) Not yet! Not yet! Not yet! Not yet! Not yet! Not yet! Not yet! Not yet! (gets to her room and closes the door, as Rachel gets up to answer the door.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello, Chip.

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: Hey, Rach! How ya doin'?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm great! I'm great. I've got a great job at Bloomingdale's, have wonderful friends, and eventhough I'm not seeing anyone right now, I've never felt better about myself.

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: So ah, Monica ready yet?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She'll be out in a second. So, Chip, how's umm, Amy Welch?

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: Amy Welch? Wow! I haven't seen her since... So, Monica about ready?
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is showing off the entertainment center.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is the unit for you my friend. Sturdy construction, tons of storage compartments, some big enough to fit a grown man.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah! I got in there myself once. My roommate bet me five bucks that I couldn't, and then he stuck a board through the handles that locked me in. Yeah. It was funny 'til I started feeling like I was in a coffin.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: No, you, you can't fit in that thing. That's not deep enough.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah? (opens up the center and takes out the stereo) If I can't, I'll knock five bucks off the price off the unit.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: All right, you have yourself a deal. (shakes his hand)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. (he gets in the unit and closes the door) See?! I told ya!
The guys takes a hockey stick and slips it through the handles then proceeds to take the stereo and Chandler's computer and walk out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sometimes I get in here just to get away from it! Hey, a nickel!!
A street, Chip is walking Monica to his motorcycle.

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: Here, we are.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! You still have the Chipper!

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: The what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's what we used to call your ah, your motorcycle in high school. Y'know how a motorcycle is a Chopper, and you're Chip. Nevermind.

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: No, I think it's cute. (kisses her)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! A lipper from Chipper.

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: So you still in touch with anyone from high school?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm. Well, there's Rachel, and umm, I think that's it. How bout you?

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: Oh yeah, I still hang with Simmons and Zana, y'know. I see Spindler a lot. Devane, Kelly, and I run into Goldie from time to time. Steve Brown, Zuchoff, McGwire, J.T., Breadsly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is that all?

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: Ehh, y'know after high school, you just kinda lose touch. Oh yeah! I ran into Richard Dorfman.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, how is he?

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: Not so good, Simmons and I gave him a wedgie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Isn't he an architect now?

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: Yeah, they still wear underwear.

Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is coming home. As we walks through the door, and without looking up, he goes to throw his keys on the foosball table, only, it's gone and the keys it the floor. He then looks up at an empty apartment; everything is gone except for the entertainment center and that ceramic dog. Even the food in the fridge!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OH MY GOD!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (still trapped in the entertainment center) WHAT?!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you all right?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (lets him out) What happened?!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (getting out) Awww, man! He promised he wouldn't take the chairs!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What the hell happened?!! How were you locked in?!! And where the hell is all of our stuff?!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, this guy came by to look at the unit and-and he said he didn't think big enough to fit a grown man!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So-You got in voluntarily?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I was tryin' to make a sale!! Oh, man, if I ever run into that guy again, do you know what I'm gonna do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: BEND OVER?!!!
Dot's Spot, Chip and Monica are on there date, eating dinner. Chip is telling a story.

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: ...and then Zana, just let one rip!! (laughs histerically)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, not that I enjoy talking about people who I went to high school with, 'cause I do, but umm, maybe we could talk about something else? Like you, I don't even know where you work?

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: You know where I work!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I do?

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: The movie theatre, you used to come in all the time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You still work at the multiplex?

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: Oh, like I'd give up that job! Free popcorn and candy, anytime I want. I can get you free posters for your room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks, I'm set. Do you still live with your parents?

lines.speaker: Chip
lines.text: Oh yeah, but I can stay out as late as I want.
Monica takes a big swig of her martini.
Chandler and Joey's, Rachel, Phoebe, and Julio are consoling Joey and Chandler.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! They really got you guys. Your T.V. The chairs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, your microwave. The stereo.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking through a deck of cards) Aww, man, he took the five of spades!! Oh, no-no-no, here it is!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Oh my God! What happened?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, umm, Joey was born, and then 28 years later, I was robbed!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica) So, how was your date?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, y'know how I always wanted to go out with Chip Matthews in high school?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, tonight, I actually went out with Chip Matthews in high school.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, it's okay, not only did I get to go out with Chip Matthews, I got to dump Chip Matthews.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! That's so great!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey! So ah, what did the insurance company say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, they said uh, "You don't have insurance here, so stop calling us."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (seeing Phoebe still with cat) You didn't tell her?! (They all kinda shy away.) Okay, fine! Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah? (sees Ross) Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! Listen uhh, this cat belongs to a little girl. There are flyers all over the place.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, sweetie. (shows her the flyer)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, we can take her back with you if you want.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh. Um-hmm. But y'know, she choose to find me. I mean, I have to respect her decision. Right?

lines.speaker: Chandler, Monica, Joey, and Rachel
lines.text: That's a good call. Right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! Look-Hey, enough is enough! Look, I am sorry that you feel guilty or whatever about spending time with your new mom, but this is not your old mom. This is a cat! Okay, Julio the cat! Not mom! Cat!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (she turns around and puts the cat on the entertainment center) Ross, how many parents have you lost?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: None.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, then you don't know what it feels like when one of them comes back. Do you? I believe this is my Mother. Even if I'm wrong, who cares? Just be a friend. Okay? Be supportive.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know what to say.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You could.... say you're sorry to her mom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I think she would like that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (goes over to Julio) Come here, here, come here, come here, (pause) Mrs. Buffay. Sorry, about what I said, umm, it was, it was insensitive of me to say that you were just a cat. When clearly you are also the reincarnated spirit, of my friend's mother.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you. We both forgive you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So honey, what are you gonna do about the little girl?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, okay, listen, umm, Mom, I hope you know you still mean a lot to me. And you're welcome to come back anytime.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs, if she could come back as a couch, we'd really appreciate it. (Joey nods in agreement)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on, Mom, I'll take you home.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'll go with you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me too. (they all leave)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Y'know, I've got an extra futon.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, you don't have to brag! We got nothing here!!
Closing Credits

Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Chandler have trading the entertainment center for the canoe. Joey is sitting in the bow, staring off into space as to envision his future full of possibilities. Chandler is sitting at the stern, staring into space and is looking at an uncertain future.
End
